element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns basic flight, attack patterns, fuel management against ground threats
Unlimited ammo baseline; introduces weapon selection concept
Limited ammo; more powerful; teaches resource management vs. utility tradeoff
Most powerful; severely limited ammo; teaches situational weapon selection
Airlifting rescued allies to landing zones restores armor; teaches risk-reward navigation
Limited armor; restored via power-ups or rescues; requires evasion and resource management
Steady fuel depletion; refuel via barrels; teaches time/distance pressure
Different handling, speed characteristics; tactical vehicle swapping
Unique terrain interaction; expands environmental puzzle-solving
Variable height, unlimited ammo, but fragile; high-risk glass cannon option
Primary air threat; requires dodging and tactical positioning
Ground threats; require appropriate weapon selection and approach tactics
Zone denial; teaches strategic approach planning and evasion
Destroy weapons/installations, rescue hostages, capture personnel; nonlinear task selection

typical_relief_triggers: Hostage/POW rescue and airlifting to landing zone
typical_tension_triggers: Anti-aircraft gun sighting and incoming fire
Entering new objective area with unknown threats
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Mission-based wave progression with interleaved resource management pressure; tension builds through threat density and resource scarcity, releases through objective completion and safe refueling/rearming

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiple simultaneous mission objectives within levels; nonlinear task selection suggests player-driven routing through space

difficulty_curve.early_game: Level 1 (Washington D.C.): Introduction to helicopter flight, basic weapon systems, anti-aircraft threats, and hostage rescue mechanics. Moderate difficulty teaching core systems.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Jungle fortress progression with expanded enemy weapon variety (tanks, artillery); introduction of alternate vehicles (motorbike, hovercraft) with different mechanics; increasing fuel/ammo pressure
difficulty_curve.late_game: Penultimate level: Dual antagonist pursuit sequences; final level reprises Washington D.C. with high-threat nuclear bomb escort interception; F-117 jet available as glass-cannon option for veteran players
notable_spikes: Introduction of F-117 stealth jet (high fragility despite powerful weapons)
Penultimate level dual antagonist hunt (coordination and positioning pressure)
Final level nuclear bomb escort (time pressure + multi-target management)

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 1 (Washington D.C.): defensive posture repelling terrorist attacks on presidential assets; teaches helicopter basics, weapon selection, and evasion in familiar urban setting
Mid-game levels advance towards antagonist stronghold through hostile terrain; expanding enemy complexity, vehicle introduction, resource scarcity
Pursuit of both Kilbaba and Ortega to separate hideouts; requires tactical prioritization and sustained engagement
Return to Washington D.C. with escaped antagonists and nuclear bomb-laden truck convoy; combines all learned mechanics under extreme time/threat pressure
Alaska level adds narrative epilogue against Russian defector Ptofski; oil tanker destruction under ecological threat framing
